content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке
Document lines: 619-628

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=One-line roles of v3 memory data model components summary=Section 8 of `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md` gives a compact glossary for the v3 memory model: `Source` is what was said or observed, `Entity` is who or what it is about, `Note` is important useful context not yet cemented as fact, `Claim` is a normalized fact, `Profile` is a compact projection of stable context, `Task` is what must be done, `Episode` is what the system learned, and `Memor...[truncated 32 chars] scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, summarizing the one-line roles of v3 memory data...[truncated 18 chars] entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1>,entity:<2>,entity:<3>,entity:<4>,entity:<5>,entity:<6>,entity:<7> keywords=Source,Entity,Note,Claim,Profile,Task,Episode,MemoryRun,one-line model,v3 memory data model tags=doc_digest,data_model,glossary evidence=- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 memory data model layered architecture summary=The `v3` memory data model is designed to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability by separating memory into layers: `Source` as evidence, `Entity`/`Claim` as graph-shaped semantics, `Note` as rich intermediate context, `Profile`/`Task`/`Episode` as operational projections, and `MemoryRun` as audit. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, describing the overall v3 memory da...[truncated 22 chars] keywords=v3,memory data model,Source,Entity,Claim,Note,Profile,Task,Episode,MemoryRun,layers tags=data_model,architecture,v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note represents durable intermediate semantic context summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment for important retrieval-useful context that should not yet be hardened into final claims, such as design directions, decision rationale, evolving plans, hypotheses, lessons, doc digests, and contextual framing. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining when to use `Note` and how it differs ...[truncated 41 chars] keywords=Note,semantic memory fragment,retrieval,design direction,decision rationale,hypotheses,doc digests tags=doc_digest,data_model,note useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Entity is the canonical memory node for aliases, merges, and semantic anchors summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies multiple mentions of the same object, anchors claims/tasks/notes/episodes, and supports aliases and merge. Its invariants require merges to preserve history, aliases to point to the current canonical entity, and mistaken merges to be reversible through an audit trail. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the role and invariants of `Entity`. keywords=Entity,canonical node,aliases,merge,entity_aliases,audit trail tags=data_model,entity,canonicalization,merge useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note and Claim serve different memory roles summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Claim` is for precise factual answers, predicate-rule reconciliation, profile updates, and time-aware status answers, while `Note` is for richer retrieval, decision history, rationale, partial or evolving meaning, and later consolidation. The document warns that storing everything as `Claim` creates false precision, while storing everything as `Note` ma...[truncated 20 chars] scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 4. Почему `Note` не заменяет `Claim`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, explaining why Note does not replace Claim. keywords=Note,Claim,false precision,soft memory,reconciliation,richer retrieval tags=doc_digest,memory_model,note_vs_claim useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note invariants require one self-contained semantic fragment summary=`Note` invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md` require each note to be one self-contained semantic fragment that adds value beyond duplicating the source; `candidate_claims_json` is only hints, and a note may remain long-lived without becoming a claim if it stays useful for retrieval. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, describing Note invariants and lifecycle expect...[truncated 7 chars] keywords=Note invariants,semantic fragment,candidate_claims_json,retrieval,source added value tags=doc_digest,data_model,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Recommended schema field semantics for Note, Claim, Task, and Profile summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, recommended Pydantic/schema descriptions define how memory fields should constrain behavior: `Note.summary` must be self-contained and preserve uncertainty, `scope_json` prevents leakage or over-generalization, `Claim.normalized_text` is a stand-alone factual statement, `Claim.retrieval_state` is search temperature distinct from truth status, `Task.accep...[truncated 123 chars] scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 7. Recommended field descriptions для schema / Pydantic` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, listing recommended sche...[truncated 58 chars] keywords=Pydantic,schema,Note.summary,scope_json,maturity_score,Claim.normalized_text,retrieval_state,Task.acceptance_criteria_json,Profile.soft_signals tags=doc_digest,schema_fields,memory_model useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Profile is a short rebuildable always-in-context projection summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Profile` is a compact always-in-context projection over explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context, not a source of truth. Its invariants emphasize keeping it short, prioritizing explicit facts over inference, leaving disputed facts in claim/note, and making the profile rebuildable. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.6 `Profile`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the role, structure, and invariants...[truncated 14 chars] keywords=Profile,always-in-context,projection layer,source of truth,stable claims,long-lived context tags=doc_digest,data_model,profile useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<7> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=PredicateCatalog configures predicate reconciliation behavior summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `PredicateCatalog` is not a memory item; it is a reconciliation configuration that captures per-predicate differences such as cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict behavior, and whether facts sync into profile or task projections. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.3 `PredicateCatalog`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the role and recommended f...[truncated 28 chars] keywords=PredicateCatalog,reconciliation,cardinality,temporal_policy,conflict_policy,profile_sync,task_sync tags=doc_digest,data_model,reconciliation useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
